criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented build_pipeline_consumer_deps in src/forge/cli/_serve_deps.py. The factory composes the three Wave-2 collaborator factories (forward_context_builder, stage_log_recorder, state_channel) plus the publisher/emitter pair, and wires four field closures: (a) forge_config passed through; (b) _build_is_duplicate_terminal returns an async closure that issues SELECT status against the (feature_id, correlation_id) unique index per ASSUM-014; (c) _build_dispatch_build calls dispatch_autobuild_async with the four wave-2 collaborators + lifecycle_emitter; (d) _build_publish_build_failed wraps PipelinePublisher.publish_build_failed. Verified by tests/cli/test_serve_deps.py::TestFactoryWiresAllFourFields (4 tests).
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented build_pipeline_consumer_deps in src/forge/cli/_serve_deps.py. The factory composes the three Wave-2 collaborator factories (forward_context_builder, stage_log_recorder, state_channel) plus the publisher/emitter pair, and wires four field closures: (a) forge_config passed through; (b) _build_is_duplicate_terminal returns an async closure that issues SELECT status against the (feature_id, correlation_id) unique index per ASSUM-014; (c) _build_dispatch_build calls dispatch_autobuild_async with the four wave-2 collaborators + lifecycle_emitter; (d) _build_publish_build_failed wraps PipelinePublisher.publish_build_failed. Verified by tests/cli/test_serve_deps.py::TestFactoryWiresAllFourFields (4 tests).

criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented in src/forge/cli/_serve_dispatcher.py: factory returns an async def dispatch(msg: _MsgLike) -> None closure that satisfies the DispatchFn type alias from forge.cli._serve_daemon. Verified by tests/cli/test_serve_dispatcher.py::TestDispatcherClosureSignature (2 tests assert inspect.iscoroutinefunction and single-positional-argument signature).
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented in src/forge/cli/_serve_dispatcher.py: factory returns an async def dispatch(msg: _MsgLike) -> None closure that satisfies the DispatchFn type alias from forge.cli._serve_daemon. Verified by tests/cli/test_serve_dispatcher.py::TestDispatcherClosureSignature (2 tests assert inspect.iscoroutinefunction and single-positional-argument signature).

criteria_verification.evidence: The dispatcher body is a single line: `await pipeline_consumer.handle_message(msg, deps)`. handle_message is intentionally looked up via the module attribute (not imported as a name) so the seam test can monkey-patch the module-level handle_message. Verified by TestDispatcherDelegatesWithoutAck (2 tests: forwards msg+deps; never calls msg.ack), TestDispatcherDoesNotAckSeam (verbatim seam test from task brief), and TestMalformedDelegation (no short-circuit on bad payloads).
criteria_verification.notes: The dispatcher body is a single line: `await pipeline_consumer.handle_message(msg, deps)`. handle_message is intentionally looked up via the module attribute (not imported as a name) so the seam test can monkey-patch the module-level handle_message. Verified by TestDispatcherDelegatesWithoutAck (2 tests: forwards msg+deps; never calls msg.ack), TestDispatcherDoesNotAckSeam (verbatim seam test from task brief), and TestMalformedDelegation (no short-circuit on bad payloads).

criteria_verification.evidence: Modified src/forge/cli/serve.py to add a compose_dispatch_chain seam (mirroring the recovery_reconcile_on_boot / consumer_reconcile_on_boot seam pattern). _run_serve now awaits compose_dispatch_chain(client) between the reconciles and state.set_chain_ready(True). The bind_production_dispatch_chain factory builds a closure that calls build_pipeline_consumer_deps + make_handle_message_dispatcher + assigns _serve_daemon.dispatch_payload = dispatcher. After this assignment runs in production, the receipt-only _default_dispatch is no longer reachable. The default no-op preserves backward compatibility for tests (TestServeCmdSmoke etc.) — they keep passing without a SQLite pool wiring.
criteria_verification.notes: Modified src/forge/cli/serve.py to add a compose_dispatch_chain seam (mirroring the recovery_reconcile_on_boot / consumer_reconcile_on_boot seam pattern). _run_serve now awaits compose_dispatch_chain(client) between the reconciles and state.set_chain_ready(True). The bind_production_dispatch_chain factory builds a closure that calls build_pipeline_consumer_deps + make_handle_message_dispatcher + assigns _serve_daemon.dispatch_payload = dispatcher. After this assignment runs in production, the receipt-only _default_dispatch is no longer reachable. The default no-op preserves backward compatibility for tests (TestServeCmdSmoke etc.) — they keep passing without a SQLite pool wiring.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `_serve_state.chain_ready` is True after the rebind; the healthz
criteria_verification.evidence: _run_serve calls compose_dispatch_chain(client) BEFORE state.set_chain_ready(True), so the rebind always precedes the chain_ready flip. SubscriptionState.is_healthy() returns live AND chain_ready, so once the daemon's pull subscription binds, healthz reports 200. The existing FW10-001 boot-order tests (test_cli_serve_skeleton.py::TestRunServeBootOrder) still pass, confirming the boot order invariant is preserved.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 293 chars provided.
